contradictions.summary: The call suggested real-time reconciliation could work before launch, while the proposal excludes real-time payment reconciliation from pilot scope.

claimA.text: We can likely get real-time reconciliation working before launch.

claimB.text: Real-time payment reconciliation is not included in pilot scope.

contradictions.recommendedResolution: Clarify that daily batch reconciliation is committed and real-time reconciliation remains Product review.

contradictions.summary: Customer expects payment token handling in the launch plan, while the signed SOW says the vendor will not receive or process payment tokens.

claimA.text: Payment token handling has to be inside the launch plan, otherwise finance will keep a manual exception queue.

claimB.text: Vendor will not receive or process payment tokens.

contradictions.recommendedResolution: Use the SOW exclusion and Product scope safe wording in all customer-facing drafts.

contradictions.summary: Support promised a connector mapping update by May 23, while the SOW states support does not create a same-day connector-change SLA.

claimA.text: Support will publish a connector mapping update by May 23 and escalate unmapped SKU families to Product Integrations.

claimB.text: Configuration support is best-effort and does not create a same-day connector-change SLA.

contradictions.recommendedResolution: Track the mapping update as a support commitment but avoid representing it as an SLA.
